canonical_case_status_verbatim.scope: the cookbook is publish-ready up to the owner gate; this verdict prices, it does not publish

_preregistration.kernel_lineage_note: the fee kernel below is the V037/V039/V040 lineage kernel (same requesting seat, same catalog); VERDICT 039's target line pre-named this inheritance verbatim: 'the cookbook PWYW question inherits this verdict's PWYW frontier form directly' (sim-lab control/outbox.md, VERDICT 039 · 2026-07-13T10:06:56Z)

committed_price_usd.source_verbatim: Price **$19 one-time** — set at intake ([`INTAKE.md`](../../../candidates/merge-wall-cookbook/INTAKE.md): "Conservative revenue estimate: $19 one-time") and recorded identically in the listing copy, the click-script, and here.

price_chain.source_verbatim: Chain: $15 (kill-rule kit, false-green) < **$19** = template-packs $19 PWYW < SWTK $29 live (PR #86) < field-manual $39 (PR #110) < membership-kit $49 (PR #106).

pwyw_precedent_in_catalog.value: template-packs is the ONLY in-catalog PWYW listing ($19 suggested) and it is NOT live (publish click queued, PR #108)
pwyw_precedent_in_catalog.source_verbatim: Price **$19 pay-what-you-want (suggested)** — precedent recorded in [`owner-actions.md`](../../launch/template-packs/owner-actions.md) ⚑D and [`candidates/template-packs/LISTING.md`](../../../candidates/template-packs/LISTING.md) ("**Pay-what-you-want, $19 suggested** (one-time, free updates to the v0.x line)").
pwyw_precedent_in_catalog.not_live_note: OWNER-QUEUE.md @ f15e9f1 still carries the unchecked click '⚑D template-packs ($19 PWYW) publish click must be executed first' — no live PWYW listing exists anywhere in the catalog, hence no PWYW measurement can exist (G1)

gumroad_direct_fees.source_verbatim: Gumroad: flat 10% platform fee + $0.50 per transaction on direct sales; payment processing (~2.9% + $0.30) brings the practical total to roughly ~12.9% + $0.80 per sale.
gumroad_direct_fees.decision_channel_note: the DECISION is taken on Gumroad direct — the cookbook's own §7 default ('⚑ Storefront pick: Gumroad (default — the click-script's HOW is written against it; same account as the SWTK live listing) or Lemon Squeezy'); Ko-fi and Discover frontiers ship as reporting columns; no Lemon Squeezy fee schedule exists in the packet (G4)

gumroad_discover_fees.source_verbatim: Products surfaced via Gumroad's Discover marketplace are charged 30% (processing included).

kofi_free_fees.source_verbatim: Ko-fi: 0% on tips; 5% on Shop / Memberships / Commissions on the free plan (plus normal ~3% + $0.30 processor fee).

fee_floor_rule.source_verbatim: Below ~$3 the fixed fees ($0.50 + ~$0.30) eat >25% of price — do not price under $3.
fee_floor_rule.reconciliation_note: exact: fixed fees 0.80/p > 25% iff p < 3.20 (16/5); a zero-minimum PWYW listing admits payments below the packet's own floor, and a payment nets NEGATIVE below p = 800/871 ≈ $0.9185 — the m = 3 floor variant honors the rule by construction

demand_expectation.source_verbatim: Conservative revenue estimate: $19 one-time. Conservative first-90-day: 0–3 sales ($0–$57). Zero distribution = $0.
demand_expectation.scale_note: no sales projection is made anywhere in this verdict; the committed 0–3 band is rendered per arm as reporting only

kill_rule.source_verbatim: Validation signal: within 30 days, ≥1 sale OR ≥30 reads from the agent-builder audience. Else ledgered negative.
kill_rule.model_note: $0-takers on a zero-minimum PWYW earn nothing and cost nothing but may serve the ≥30-reads leg — modeled as a separate measured output (taker counts), never as revenue (A4)

tam_and_wtp.tam_verbatim: High-intent but small TAM (people building self-merging agent fleets); rides the same owner-gated r/ClaudeAI-type community funnel as the Field Manual — does not diversify channel risk.
tam_and_wtp.wtp_verbatim: Narrow but real pain; those who have it have few alternatives, so moderate WTP + some moat.
tam_and_wtp.source: venture-lab candidates/merge-wall-cookbook/INTAKE.md @ f15e9f1, scoring table (Distribution / WTP rows)

no_sales_data_anywhere.source_verbatim: Payback-time estimate: unverified until first sale; owner-gated distribution.
no_sales_data_anywhere.corroboration: vetting §7 verdict line @ f15e9f1: 'a live purchase remains unverified until the owner's own test purchase'; the only live listing in the catalog is SWTK $29 (fixed price) with no reported sales; the only PWYW listing (template-packs) is not live — G1 is total

assumed_not_packet_pinned: A1 PWYW MECHANICS: a PWYW taker either pays w ≥ minimum or takes at $0 (zero-minimum variant only); per-transaction fees apply only to paid transactions; a $0 take nets exactly $0 (no fee owed on a $0 transaction — not packet-pinned, marked ASSUMED; identical to V039 A3).

A2 PROCESSOR RATE FIXED at the packet's own cited values (~2.9% + $0.30 direct; ~3% + $0.30 Ko-fi). The packet itself says 'per-sale net is exact only for a given processor rate' — a ±0.6pp processing-rate bracket ships as reporting.

A3 GRID SPANS ARE REGISTERED BANDS, NOT MEASUREMENTS: u ∈ [0, 3] (paid PWYW transactions per baseline $19-fixed buyer), w̄ ∈ [0, 29] (mean paid amount; upper bound = the catalog's next price rung, SWTK $29 live), z ∈ {0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9} ($0-taker share, reads-leg reporting only) bracket every packet-plausible value; no probability mass is claimed anywhere on them.

A4 DOWNLOADS-AS-READS: whether a $0 PWYW download counts toward the intake's '≥30 reads' validation leg is the seat's call — the sim reports exact taker-count arithmetic, never asserts the equivalence.
A5 MECHANISM-NEUTRAL TRAFFIC: switching the listing's price mechanism does not itself change listing traffic; any PWYW-side entry-rate lift IS the unmeasured u — no double-counting.
recorded_gaps_feeding_null_or_conditiona: G1 (CRITICAL): NO measured PWYW conversion, taker-rate, or mean-payment data exists anywhere in the repo, and zero organic sales data exists for ANY product (intake verbatim 'unverified until first sale'; the catalog's only PWYW listing, template-packs $19 suggested, is NOT live). The exact mirror of V039's G1, verified again at f15e9f1.
G2: NO absolute demand data — the packet's own scale is the intake's '0–3 sales ($0–$57)' first-90-day band with 'Zero distribution = $0'; no sales projection is made anywhere in this verdict.
G3: processor rates are cited approximations ('~2.9%', '~3%') — the packet's own exactness caveat; bracketed in a reporting column (A2).

G4: the storefront pick is an owner call (Gumroad default vs Lemon Squeezy, vetting §7 step 2); NO Lemon Squeezy fee schedule exists in the packet — if the owner picks it, every frontier recomputes from its schedule with the same affine form; Ko-fi/Discover kernels are committed and ship as reporting columns.
G5: the product is publish-ready but NOT live — the $19 was set at intake and the publish click is owner-gated (vetting §7); this verdict prices, it does not publish and cannot unblock any click.
G6: no reads/downloads telemetry exists for the kill rule's ≥30-reads leg — the reads-leg arithmetic ships as exact bars on unmeasured quantities (A4).

model.frame: expected net revenue per committed baseline buyer (one buyer who would buy the cookbook at the committed $19 fixed on the decision channel — the V039 frame with the cookbook's constants) over the intake's first-90-day window; exact rational arithmetic (fractions of USD) on every decision quantity

arms.FIXED_19: the committed default: $19 one-time fixed. E[net] = net(19) per baseline buyer = 15749/1000 = $15.749 exact on Gumroad direct. Zero unmeasured parameters on a given channel.
arms.PWYW_MIN0: pay-what-you-want, $19 suggested, minimum $0: u = paid PWYW transactions per baseline buyer, w̄ = mean paid amount, z = $0-taker share. E[net] = u·net(w̄) by linearity of the affine kernel over the paid-payment distribution ($0 takes net $0, A1; the linearity step is exact for ANY payment mix with mean w̄, not an approximation). u, w̄, z all unmeasured (G1).
arms.PWYW_MIN3: PWYW with minimum floor $3 (the smallest floor honoring the packet's own fee-floor rule): same form with w̄ ≥ 3 enforced by the listing.
arms.PWYW_MIN5: PWYW with minimum floor $5: same form with w̄ ≥ 5.
arms.PWYW_MIN19: PWYW with minimum floor $19 (= $19 fixed with voluntary overpay): net per paid sale ≥ net(19) for every payment, so it weakly dominates FIXED_19 PER PAID SALE by construction; its entire risk is conversion-side (u < 1 if the 'minimum $19' framing deters vs a plain $19 price) — u unmeasured (G1). The mirror of V039's parked PWYW-with-$5-floor.

model.core_frontier: PWYW ≥ FIXED_19 on a channel with kernel net(·) ⇔ u·net(w̄) ≥ net(19) (paying takers only; z drops out of revenue entirely). On Gumroad direct: u·(0.871·w̄ − 0.80) ≥ 15.749.
model.kernel_invariance_theorem: for ANY affine kernel net(p) = a·p − b with a > 0: at u = 1 the flip requires a·w̄ − b ≥ a·19 − b ⇔ w̄ ≥ $19.00 EXACTLY — the parity bar is kernel-invariant, identical on direct/Ko-fi/Discover/any future channel; PWYW can only win via u > 1 outrunning both the sub-$19 payment mix and the per-transaction fixed-fee drag (each extra paid transaction costs exactly b more; direct b = $0.80)

sweep_grids.pwyw_grid: u ∈ 0.00..3.00 step 0.10 (31) × w̄ ∈ 0.00..29.00 step 0.50 (59) = 1829 cells, WIN map PWYW vs FIXED_19, all three committed channels (decision column: Gumroad direct); twin evaluators per cell (Fraction expected-value comparison vs integer cross-multiplication inequality)
sweep_grids.floor_masks: for m ∈ {0, 3, 5, 19}: the pwyw_grid restricted to w̄ ≥ m — per-floor exact minimum-u bar u_min(m) = net(19)/net(m) and constrained win-region count
sweep_grids.split_ladder: k ∈ 1..5 paid takers gross-splitting one $19 (equal payments 19/k, all above the active floor where applicable): total net = 0.871·19 − k·0.80 on direct — the exact per-transaction fee drag, REPORTING (it is the mechanism behind the frontier, not a separate decision)
sweep_grids.reads_leg: z ∈ {0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9} × the committed 0–3 baseline-buyer band: total downloads per baseline buyer = u/(1−z); exact bar for the kill rule's ≥30-reads leg: B baseline buyers in the 30-day window need u/(1−z) ≥ 30/B — REPORTING only (A4, G6)
sweep_grids.processing_sensitivity: REPORTING-ONLY, cannot flip (kernel-invariance: the u = 1 bar is w̄ = $19 at ANY affine kernel): direct processing rate ∈ {2.3%, 2.9%, 3.5%} — re-renders net(19) per rate
sweep_grids.scale_scenarios: REPORTING-ONLY: the intake's own first-90-day band rendered per arm — n19 ∈ {0, 1, 2, 3} baseline buyers (gross $0/$19/$38/$57)

decision_rule.note: pre-registered BEFORE the runner; evaluated strictly in order; all breakevens exact fractions on the Gumroad-direct decision channel
order: R1 MEASURED-PWYW-WINS: iff the packet carries a MEASURED pair (û, ŵ̄) with û·net(ŵ̄) ≥ 15749/1000 on the decision channel → replace the committed $19 fixed with PWYW. (Registered though unreachable by the packet's own text — G1 says no PWYW measurement exists anywhere; kept so a future packet re-reads.)

R2 FULL-BAND-DOMINANCE: iff one arm weakly dominates the other across the ENTIRE registered (u, w̄) band on the decision channel → approve the dominant arm outright. (Expected structurally FALSE for every unmeasured-u variant — both win regions non-empty; the runner must prove it by exact count. PWYW_MIN19 dominates per PAID SALE but not per baseline buyer — its u is free on [0, 3] — so it cannot fire R2 either.)

R3 CONDITIONAL-DEFAULT: iff NO measured PWYW/conversion datum exists (G1 open) AND no arm full-band dominates → keep the committed **$19 one-time fixed** (the only arm with zero unmeasured parameters, committed at intake + vetting §3 + the precedent chain, and holding the catalog's $19 rung shared with template-packs); PWYW (any sub-$19 floor) PARKS behind the exact measured frontier u·net(w̄) ≥ 15.749 with named measurement bars; PWYW_MIN19 ships as the one variant that cannot lose per paid sale, parked on its conversion-side risk (G1).
R4 NULL: anything else — name the binding gap.
decision_rule.honest_null_note: R3 and R4 are both honest outcomes; the frontier tables are the citable pin either way. No sales projection is made anywhere (the intake's '0–3 sales ($0–$57) / Zero distribution = $0' stands).

expected_exact_values_hand_derived.pwyw_parity_wbar_at_u1_all_channels: w̄* = 19 EXACTLY on direct, Ko-fi, and Discover (kernel-invariance theorem — same affine kernel on both sides)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.u_min_at_wbar3_direct: 15749/1813 ≈ 8.6867 (PWYW averaging the $3 floor needs ~8.69× taker volume)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.split_ladder_direct: k paid takers gross-splitting $19 net 16.549 − 0.80k: k=1 → 15.749 · k=2 → 14.949 · k=3 → 14.149 · k=4 → 13.349 · k=5 → 12.549 (each extra transaction costs exactly $0.80 — the fee drag PWYW's u > 1 must outrun)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.zero_profit_payment_floor_direct: a paid PWYW take nets POSITIVE only above p = 800/871 ≈ 0.9185; the packet's own fee-floor rule crossing is p = 16/5 = 3.20 (fixed-fee share 0.80/p = 25%); at $3 the share is 4/15 ≈ 26.67%, at $5 16%, at $19 4/95 ≈ 4.21%
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.processing_bracket_net19_direct: 2.3% → 15.863 · 2.9% → 15.749 · 3.5% → 15.635 (the u = 1 parity bar stays $19.00 at every rate — cannot flip)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.reads_leg_bars: downloads per baseline buyer = u/(1−z); ≥30 downloads in the 30-day kill window from B baseline buyers needs u/(1−z) ≥ 30/B — at the band's full 3 buyers and u = 1 that is z ≥ 0.9 (a 10× download multiplier); at 1 buyer, z ≥ 29/30 ≈ 0.9667 (30×)

seeds.allocation_note: ZERO seeds drawn — every decision quantity and every gate in this sim is a closed-form exact Fraction (the V037/V038 degenerate-case precedent: 'if fully exact, zero seed draws is the honest choice'); the fleet seed high-water stands at 20261280 (claimed by VERDICT 043's robustness leg, recorded at origin/main 68c9317) and is UNTOUCHED by this verdict. seeds.scope: with no RNG anywhere, byte-identity across runs is by construction and is still proven by external diff per the determinism contract

determinism_contract: stdout and results.json byte-identical across two full process runs, proven by external diff; all JSON keys sorted; no timestamps inside results.json; stdlib-only; the sim reads exactly ONE file (this fixtures.json)
